concepts.name: Gradient Synchronization
concepts.description: The mathematical necessity of ensuring all distributed worker GPUs apply identical weight updates to maintain a consistent global model state.

concepts.name: AllReduce
concepts.description: A collective communication primitive where all nodes share and aggregate their local arrays, leaving every node with the identical final reduced result.

concepts.name: All-to-All Communication
concepts.description: A communication pattern where every node sends distinct, targeted data partitions to specific destination nodes, commonly used in Mixture of Experts architectures.

concepts.name: Process Group
concepts.description: A specific subset of GPUs in a cluster that exclusively participates in a designated collective communication operation.

concepts.name: Bandwidth-Distance Product
concepts.description: A physical constraint dictating that a network link can provide either high data throughput or cover long physical distances, but not both cheaply.

concepts.name: Remote Direct Memory Access
concepts.description: A networking technology that bypasses the operating system kernel, allowing network cards to read directly from memory to drastically reduce per-message latency.

concepts.name: GPUDirect RDMA
concepts.description: An advanced hardware configuration that allows a network interface card to transfer data directly to and from GPU high-bandwidth memory over the PCIe bus without CPU involvement.

concepts.name: Alpha-Beta Performance Model
concepts.description: A linear network cost model that computes total data transfer time as a function of fixed startup latency and sustained per-byte bandwidth.

concepts.name: Critical Message Size
concepts.description: The specific data payload threshold at which a network communication operation shifts from being bounded by latency to being bounded by bandwidth.

concepts.name: Ring AllReduce
concepts.description: A bandwidth-optimized collective algorithm that arranges GPUs in a logical circle, breaking data into chunks to maximize network utilization without bottlenecking a single node.

concepts.name: Hierarchical AllReduce
concepts.description: A multi-level communication strategy that applies different collective algorithms to optimize traffic across distinct hardware topologies, such as using ring within a node and trees across nodes.

concepts.name: Gradient Compression
concepts.description: Reducing the size of the communication payload by quantizing or sparsifying gradient tensors before transmitting them across the network.

concepts.name: Error Feedback
concepts.description: A mathematical technique used alongside compression to accumulate and apply truncation errors in subsequent training steps, preventing model divergence.

concepts.name: Communication-Computation Overlap
concepts.description: A performance optimization that schedules network data transfers to occur simultaneously with independent mathematical computations, effectively hiding communication latency.

concepts.name: Bucket Fusion
concepts.description: The practice of grouping multiple small gradient tensors into larger contiguous memory buffers before transmission to minimize network startup latency taxes.

concepts.name: 3D Parallelism
concepts.description: The simultaneous application of data, tensor, and pipeline parallelism to successfully train massive frontier-scale machine learning models.
